name: Eggplant-Mushroom Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup dry red or white wine or vermouth
1 1/2 pounds eggplant, unpeeled, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a wide-bottomed pan or skillet with high sides over medium-high heat, heat the olive oil. Add onion and garlic; saute about 2 minutes.
2. Break tomatoes into bits. Add them with their juice, wine, eggplant, mushrooms, herbs, and sugar to the pan.
3. Cover the pan and simmer for 5 minutes. Remove the cover and cook over medium heat until the sauce reduces and thickens, 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Cool to room temperature and refrigerate or freeze (see Timesaver Tip).
5. To serve: Cook 12 ounces dried spaghetti according to package directions. Drain. Serve the sauce over pasta. Serve with a generous amount of freshly grated Parmesan cheese.
Make 5 to 6 cups (5 to 6 servings).
Timesaver Tip: Sauce can be made up to 2 days ahead, covered, and refrigerated, or it can be frozen. To freeze, spoon into freezer container, cover, label, and freeze at 0 degrees F for up to 4 months. To serve, defrost sauce in microwave oven on Defrost setting for 20 to 30 minutes, stirring several times, or thaw in refrigerator for 8 to 24 hours. Gently warm the sauce in a saucepan or in microwave oven at 50% power for 3 to 5 minutes, stirring several times.
